title: ICT Order Blocks Explained
seoTitle: ICT Order Blocks Explained Guide — Master Smart Money Entries
seoDescription: Our ICT order blocks explained guide shows beginners how to spot institutional footprints, time entries with precision, and trade with smart money — not against it.
headline: The ICT Order Blocks Explained Guide That Finally Makes Smart Money Click
subheadline: Stop guessing where price will reverse. Learn how to read the footprints institutional traders leave behind — and use order blocks to enter trades with sniper-level precision.

keyPoints.title: What Is an ICT Order Block?
keyPoints.text: An ICT order block is the last up-candle before a bearish move, or the last down-candle before a bullish move — the exact zone where banks and institutions placed their orders. Price returns to these zones to pair remaining unfilled orders before continuing the intended direction. Understanding this mechanic is the foundation of trading with smart money instead of being the liquidity they consume.

keyPoints.title: Why Order Blocks Change Everything
keyPoints.text: Retail traders rely on lagging indicators that repaint or fail in trending markets — order blocks give you a structural, price-action-based reason to enter. Because institutions must return to these zones to fill their positions, order blocks offer high-probability, low-risk trade locations. This edge transforms random setups into repeatable confluences backed by how the market actually moves.

keyPoints.title: How to Trade an Order Block Step-by-Step
keyPoints.text: Start on the higher timeframe (Daily or 4H) to identify a valid order block following a strong displacement candle and a break of structure. Drop to the 15-minute or 5-minute chart to watch for price to retrace into that zone with a refined entry model — like a Fair Value Gap or a rejection wick. Set your stop loss just beyond the order block's wick and target the next area of liquidity for a clean risk-to-reward trade.

keyPoints.title: Common Mistakes Beginners Make
keyPoints.text: The most costly mistake is marking every candle before a move as a valid order block — without confirming displacement, a break of structure, and the correct market structure context, you are trading noise. Another common error is ignoring the higher timeframe bias and taking counter-trend order blocks that get steamrolled. Always validate your block with confluence: time of day, liquidity targets, and premium or discount pricing.

keyPoints.title: Your Next Step With R2F Trading
keyPoints.text: Understanding order blocks intellectually is step one — building the screen time and structured practice to execute them consistently is where most traders stall. Harvest Wright's R2F Trading coaching program walks you through real chart examples, live trade breakdowns, and a clear curriculum built on ICT methodology. Join the community at r2ftrading.com and start trading with a proven framework behind every entry.
